title: Intensely Nutty Japanese Chicken Karaage
description: A nutty, tender, and cozy Japanese dish featuring clear honey and double-fry technique, made in a kazan cauldron with Mediterranean-friendly ingredients.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, clear honey, sesame oil, grated ginger, garlic powder, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the chicken thighs to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
In a separate bowl, mix together potato starch, chopped peanuts, and sesame seeds.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and dredge in the potato starch mixture, shaking off excess.
Heat about 2-3 inches of sesame oil in a kazan cauldron to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Double-fry the chicken by frying at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 5 minutes, then increasing the heat to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and frying for an additional 5-7 minutes or until golden brown.
Drain the fried chicken on paper towels and serve hot.
Garnish with additional sesame seeds and chopped peanuts if desired.
